frames: The agent stands upright with arms bent, hands near the chest, and feet flat on the ground.
It begins to lean forward slightly, extending its arms forward and slightly downward.
The agent leans further forward at the hips, and its right leg starts to lift and bend at the knee.
Its upper body continues to tilt forward, with arms extended, while the right leg lifts higher.
The agent's torso is now nearly horizontal, with arms outstretched, balancing on its straight left leg.
It loses balance, falling forward with its upper body dropping below horizontal, and the left leg begins to bend.
The agent is actively falling, with its body angled sharply downwards and arms still extended forward.
The agent has fallen completely, lying prone and stretched out on the checkered ground.
